former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}} listed in the '{{ill||lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill||lt=heihaku envoys|UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092}}), due to the {{ill||lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたからすしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたからすしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11390679}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|shrine in Uda, Nara|same-name shrine in Kashihara, Nara|Yatagarasu Shrine (Kashihara)}} {{神社 |名称 = Yatagarasu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたからすしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたからすしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11390679}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|shrine in Uda, Nara|same-name shrine in Kashihara, Nara|Yatagarasu Shrine (Kashihara)}} {{神社 |名称 = Yatagarasu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたからすしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11390679}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|shrine in Uda, Nara|same-name shrine in Kashihara, Nara|Yatagarasu Shrine (Kashihara)}} {{神社 |名称 = Yatagarasu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440|1=Military god}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246|1=Kyōto}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969|1=Haibara Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたからすしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440|1=Military god}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246|1=Kyōto}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969|1=Haibara Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたからすしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

link|Q11390679}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|shrine in Uda, Nara|same-name shrine in Kashihara, Nara|Yatagarasu Shrine (Kashihara)}} {{神社 |名称 = Yatagarasu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440|1=Military god}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246|1=Kyōto}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969|1=Haibara Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440|1=Military god}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246|1=Kyōto}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969|1=Haibara Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]]

link|Q11390679}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|shrine in Uda, Nara|same-name shrine in Kashihara, Nara|Yatagarasu Shrine (Kashihara)}} {{神社 |名称 = Yatagarasu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440|1=Military god}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246|1=Kyōto}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969|1=Haibara Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:宇陀市の建築物]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]]

Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440|1=Military god}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246|1=Kyōto}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969|1=Haibara Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uda, Nara]] [[Category:宇陀市の歴史]]

link|Q11390679}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|shrine in Uda, Nara|same-name shrine in Kashihara, Nara|Yatagarasu Shrine (Kashihara)}} {{神社 |名称 = Yatagarasu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440|1=Military god}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246|1=Kyōto}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969|1=Haibara Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uda, Nara]] [[Category:History of Uda, Nara]]

link|Q11390679}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|shrine in Uda, Nara|same-name shrine in Kashihara, Nara|Yatagarasu Shrine (Kashihara)}} {{神社 |名称 = Yatagarasu Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}Haibara Takatsuka 42 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|10.8|N|135|56|47.2|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yatagarasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Yatagarasu
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Yatagarasu-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|Entrance torii]] '''Yatagarasu Shrine''' (やたがらすじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda|Q863747|WD=Q863747|1=Uda, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=small Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} listed in the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}}'. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. === Enshrined deity === It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} ({{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}}). === History === In the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Monmu|Q357246|WD=Q357246|1=Emperor Monmu}}, there is an entry that in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|WD=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|WD=Q23504|1=705}}), Yatagarasu-sha was worshiped, and this is taken as the founding of this shrineEntry for day bōjutsu (9th) in 9th month.. In documentary terms, it is the first shrine in Japan to worship Yatagarasu as a deity. Also, according to the 'Shinsen Shōjiroku,' when {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} moved east to Yamato, Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto transformed into "Yatagarasu" and guided the stalled imperial party through the mountains of {{ill|lt=Kumano|Q6443450|WD=Q6443450|1=Kumano region}} to Yamato, contributing to imperial victory. Because of this history, it was revered since ancient times as a {{ill|lt=war deity|Q11637440|WD=Q11637440|1=Military god}}, and in the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the shrine is said to have greatly prospered under deep devotion of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|WD=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}"Brief Origin of Yatagarasu Shrine," Bunsei 13 year of Kanoe-Tora, Ōe no Toshitsune.. However, after decline of the {{ill|lt=Southern Court (Japan)|Q3001082|WD=Q3001082|lt=Southern Court|1=Southern Court}} and repeated war damage, it fell near abolition until the mid-{{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, and it is said "yatagarasu" became colloquially slurred to "otogorosu"Hayashi Sōho, 'Washū Kyūseki Yūkō,' Enpō 9.. Later, in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} years of the Edo period, priests of {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo-sha) in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q740246|WD=Q740246|1=Kyōto}} took notice, and through their efforts and cooperation of local volunteers, revival was achieved. It is said the honden was rebuilt into current {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style around this time (the stone small shrine of the former honden still remains beside the honden). After that, for annual grand festivals, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=heihaku envoys|1=UNKNOWN}} were sent from Shimogamo-sha and rites held, but because such grand rites with envoys were too heavy a burden for local parishioners then numbering only about nine households, and due guidance from Nara prefectural authorities concerned the shrine might be treated as an outside-precinct massha of Shimogamo-sha, Shimogamo participation was discontinued in early Meiji"Uda District Historical Materials.". Later, in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1917|Q2092|WD=Q2092|1=1917}}), due to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine Merger Ordinance|1=UNKNOWN}}, nearby guardian shrines came to be worshiped here, causing sudden revival. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}, related to {{ill|lt=2600th Imperial Year commemorative events|Q5366450|WD=Q5366450|1=2600th Anniversary Celebrations of the Japanese Empire}}, it was promoted to kensha, and precinct expansion and improvement were carried out. It has continued to the present. === Transportation === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Haibara Station|Q5638969|WD=Q5638969|1=Haibara Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545|lt=Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} * Parking: available === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Yatagarasu-jinja (Uda, Nara)}} *[http://www.yatagarasujinja.net/ Yatagarasu Shrine] (official site) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatagarasu Shrine]][[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Uda, Nara]] [[Category:History of Uda, Nara]]
